# Testing checklist

The crate ships a programming-model AMQP mapping, not an AMQP-wire client driver. Below is the protocol smoke checklist a downstream integration test should cover when a wire-level driver (`lapin` / `amqprs`) is wired in.

## Programming-model coverage (in-tree, `cargo test`)

| AMQP operation       | Test name | Status |
| -------------------- | --------- | ------ |
| `queue.declare`      | `declare_consumer_and_publish_default_exchange` | green |
| `exchange.publish` (direct) | `declare_consumer_and_publish_default_exchange` | green |
| `exchange.publish` (topic + `*`) | `topic_exchange_wildcard_routing` | green |
| `exchange.publish` (topic + `#`) | `topic_hash_matches_zero_or_more` | green |
| `exchange.publish` (fanout) | `fanout_exchange_broadcasts` | green |
| `exchange.publish` (headers all-of) | `headers_all_must_match` | green |
| `basic.qos prefetch` | `channel_declares_consumer_with_qos_prefetch` | green |
| `basic.nack`         | `nack_widens_offset` | green |
| Default direct exchange (`""`) | `declare_consumer_and_publish_default_exchange` | green |
| Stoplight Lean predicate | `stoplight_ext_constructors_pin_lean_predicate` | green |
| Unmatched publish dropped | `unmatched_publish_drops_message` | green |

Plus the bench (`cargo bench -p gnosis-antiqueue --bench antiqueue_vs_rabbit`) which is itself a 50,000-message ack receipt.

## Wire-level smoke (post-driver, out-of-tree)

When a wire driver lands, this list defines "the AMQP shape works end-to-end":

1. `lapin::Connection::connect("amqp://localhost:5672/")` returns a connection. (Today: there is no listener.)
2. `channel.queue_declare("tasks", QueueDeclareOptions::default(), FieldTable::default())` succeeds; subsequent `basic_publish` on the same queue routes to the declared consumer.
3. `channel.basic_qos(8, BasicQosOptions::default())` reduces in-flight to 8 per channel. (Mapping: `Stoplight::green = 8`.)
4. `channel.basic_publish("", "tasks", ...)` lands a message; the consumer's handler fires; auto-ack confirmed on the wire (no explicit `basic.ack` frame needed).
5. A handler returning Err produces a `basic.nack` on the wire; the next publish to the same consumer is delayed by one tick (phase feedback witness).
6. Topic exchange with `logs.error.*` routes a publish on `logs.error.fatal` but not `logs.info.boot`.
7. Fanout exchange with two bound queues delivers each publish to both.

## Reproducing the bench

```
cargo run --release --bin 0q -- bench         # 50k messages
cargo run --release --bin 0q -- bench 100000  # 100k messages
cargo bench -p gnosis-antiqueue --bench antiqueue_vs_rabbit
```

Numbers vary a few percent per run; the **ratio** is stable. If your p99 ratio drops below 10×, file a regression -- that is the DoD contract.
